topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights felt like physical blows, illuminating the canvas where the betrayal festered. My eyes locked onto the intricate blackwork, a pattern that mirrored the very composition of my latest piece—a piece I had poured weeks of soul into. Beneath the delicate shading of the charcoal, a stark, undeniable tattoo was visible, a signature I recognized instantly. A cold, hard knot tightened in my chest, swiftly hardening into something sharp and brittle. I didn't scream; the sound caught in my throat, a silent, internal grinding. I slowly turned from the painting, my gaze sweeping the room, cataloging every patron, every critic, searching for the source of this deliberate mockery. My hands clenched into fists, the knuckles white against the silk of my sleeve. This wasn't just theft; this was an invasion, and I felt a sudden, intense need to dismantle whatever structure had allowed this trespass.

[story 2]

I traced the line of the ink with a trembling fingertip, the sensation electric and deeply unsettling. It was a deliberate signature, placed with surgical precision over the very essence of my creation. A slow, simmering heat began to rise in my core, pushing against my skin like molten metal. I couldn't look away. Every instinct screamed for retaliation, a visceral urge to make the perpetrator understand the depth of the transgression. I walked toward the artist, my steps measured and heavy, each footfall echoing the building pressure inside me. When I finally stopped before them, my voice was low, dangerously steady. "Tell me," I managed, the words tasting like ash, "who authorized this mark upon my work?" The silence that followed was thick, suffocating, and utterly damning.

[story 3]

The discovery hit me like a physical shockwave. Staring at the embedded pigment, a profound sense of injustice flooded my system, instantly overriding all composure. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. A slow, deliberate flush crept up my neck, warming my skin with a furious energy. I didn't speak immediately. Instead, I moved with a predatory grace, circling the display, my eyes burning holes into the person responsible. My jaw locked so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. When I finally addressed them, my tone was dangerously soft, laced with an unmistakable threat. "You have taken something that was mine," I stated, my voice vibrating with suppressed intensity. "You will learn the cost of touching what belongs to another."
